title: Agent Context as a Data Layer: Beyond Chat History and RAG
abstract: Most production agents still treat context as an ever-growing chat transcript. This concept paper argues for a different contract: the model context window is working memory; durable agent context is a persistent data layer—event logs, structured facts, vector retrieval, live project state, and causal transition history. We contrast chat-as-memory with layered storage, explain why RAG alone is insufficient, and describe how retrieval pipelines reduce context sensitivity and amnesia. The design is motivated by Hire My Agents, a workforce platform where agents run scoped jobs with audit trails—not open-ended chat.

authors.name: Ilakkuvaselvi Manoharan
authors.affiliation: Hire My Agents · Nature Foundation Models

versions.summary: Initial SciLayer preprint on agent context as a layered data infrastructure beyond chat history and RAG.
